Tiananmen is the entrance gate of the imperial palace complex in Beijing. It is a national symbol of the People's Republic of China. First build in 1420 Tiananmen was the entrance to the Emperor's residence. In 1949 Mao Zedong proclaimed the People's Republic of China from the balcony.
Santa Maria del Fiore is the cathedral of the Catholic Archdiocese of Florence. It is one of world's largest churches and its dome is still the largest masonry dome ever constructed.
Machu Picchu is a 15th-century Inca citadel located in the Eastern Cordillera of southern Peru on a mountain ridge at 2430 metres.
Moai are monolithic human figures carved from stone by the Rapa Nui people between the years 1250 and 1500.
Hungarian Parliament Building is the seat of the National Assembly of Hungary. A notable landmark and a popular tourist destination in Budapest.
Mount Fuji is an active volcano located 100 km southwest of Tokyo. It is the highest mountain in Japan with a summit elevation of 3776 metres.

Pontcysyllte Aqueduct is the longest aqueduct in Great Britian and the highest canal aqueduct in the world. It carries the Llangollen Canal across the River Dee in the Vale of Llangollen in northeast Wales.
El Castillo also known as the Temple of Kukulcan is a Mesoamerican step-pyramid in the Chichen Itza archaeological site. Built by the Maya civilization.
Himeji Castle is a hiltop Japanese castle complex situated in Himeji a city in Hyogo Prefecture. The castle is regarded as the finest surviving example of prototypical Japanese castle architecture.

Stonehenge is a prehistoric megalithic structure on Sailsbury Plain in Wiltshire England. One of the most famous landmarks in the UK. It is regarded as a British curtural icon. It is speculated that Stonehenge could have been a burial ground from its earliest beginnings around 3000 BC.

A breathtaking view of the Alhambra Palace in Granada during sunset, where the purple sky blends with the warm golden lights of its historic Andalusian architecture. I chose this place because I deeply love Al-Andalus and its rich heritage. It reminds me of a stolen chapter of Islamic history—a broken piece in the heart of our civilization. For me, Alhambra symbolizes the “lost Andalusia,” carrying a quiet dream that its authentic story and identity may one day be restored to its rightful people.

A peaceful view of Trabzon overlooking the Black Sea mountains, with traditional wooden houses scattered across green hills and clouds resting on the peaks. I chose this place because its calm nature and dramatic landscapes feel like an escape from the noise of the world. I also love it because it reminds me of traditional Turkish series—the village life, the wooden homes, and the beautiful culture. Trabzon makes me imagine walking through the tea fields in their traditional clothing, especially since this region is famous for tea production, and I personally love tea a lot.

A magical sunset view over the Masai Mara savannah, with an elevated deck overlooking endless golden grasslands. I chose this place because it feels like a scene from a dream—open skies, warm colors, and a peaceful horizon stretching as far as the eye can see. The combination of African wildlife, luxury lodges, and quiet nature makes it one of the most breathtaking destinations in the world. The warm tones and the sense of freedom in the landscape give me a feeling of adventure and serenity at the same time.

A peaceful Japanese garden in Kyoto during peak autumn, with vibrant red and orange maple leaves surrounding a traditional stone path and lantern. I chose this place because Japan’s autumn has always fascinated me—the colors feel alive, warm, and full of emotion. This setting, with its soft light and elegant details, makes me feel like an ancient Japanese princess from the old eras, just like the historical films we grew up watching. Kyoto carries a sense of quiet tradition and spiritual beauty, making this scene feel timeless and magical.

A dreamy night view of Amsterdam’s canals, with warm yellow lights reflecting on the water and traditional Dutch houses lining both sides of the bridge. I chose this place because its nighttime atmosphere feels magical and calm—like a European fairytale. The reflections, bicycles, narrow streets, and glowing windows all create a cozy and romantic mood. Amsterdam at night always gave me a sense of peaceful wandering and soft city lights.

A stunning view of Lake Como with its colorful lakeside houses, calm blue water, and surrounding green mountains. I chose this place because it feels like the perfect blend of elegance, nature, and European charm. The peaceful boats, small villages, and warm Italian atmosphere make the scene look straight out of a romantic movie. Lake Como gives me a feeling of serenity and beauty—simple, calm, and effortlessly luxurious.

A magical fantasy-like garden filled with waterfalls, stone pathways, tropical trees, and soft mist that makes the place feel unreal. I chose this destination because it looks like a scene from a dream or a fantasy movie. The atmosphere reminds me of enchanted forests and hidden worlds, and the details of the rocks, water, and greenery feel almost too perfect to be real. This place gives me a sense of childlike wonder and curiosity—like discovering a secret world full of beauty and imagination.

A beautiful panoramic view of Edinburgh’s historic Old Town, with its stone buildings, narrow streets, and the iconic clock tower rising above the city. I chose this place because its atmosphere feels like stepping into a medieval European story—old architecture, cozy streets, and a soft, classic mood. Edinburgh also reminds me of the magical aesthetic of Harry Potter, with its fantasy-like streets and mysterious charm. The city feels both historical and cinematic, giving me a sense of calm curiosity mixed with a dreamy, fictional vibe.

A breathtaking night scene from the Yi Peng Lantern Festival in Chiang Mai, where thousands of glowing lanterns rise together into the dark sky. I chose this place because it feels incredibly emotional and cinematic—the kind of moment that makes you pause and take a deep breath. This festival represents one of my biggest dreams, and it always reminds me of the floating lantern scene from Rapunzel. It carries the same sense of magic, hope, and the belief that our dreams can truly come to life someday. The view is so enchanting and otherworldly that it feels almost unreal—beautiful enough to take your breath away.

A magical winter scene from Finnish Lapland, with glowing glass igloos surrounded by snow and the Northern Lights dancing across the sky. I chose this destination because the aurora has always felt like one of the most unreal and heavenly phenomena on Earth. The mix of cold air, quiet snow, and shimmering green lights creates a moment that feels spiritual, peaceful, and deeply dreamlike. Staying in a warm glass cabin while watching the sky move above me is something I’ve always dreamed of experiencing—an escape into pure nature and pure magic.
